goal: Create a first local web-app version that turns pasted French text into MP3 audiobook files using server-side ElevenLabs calls and saves them to a configured OneDrive-synced directory.
non_goals: Hosted OneDrive upload via Microsoft Graph
constraints: Never expose or return the ElevenLabs API key.
Generated MP3s must be saved to ONEDRIVE_AUDIO_DIR during local runs.
Use environment variables for secrets and configurable defaults.
Keep the first version small and reviewable.
architecture_notes: The existing Python generator owns validation, ElevenLabs request shape, output directory validation, filename sanitization, and file writing.
The first app layer exposes a local HTTP UI and JSON API around the generator without committing secrets.
Vercel deployment remains a future architecture task because direct local OneDrive writes are not available from hosted serverless functions.

tasks.title: Ship local web app v1
tasks.description: Add a minimal local web interface and API for generating French audiobook MP3s from pasted text using the existing generator.
acceptance_criteria: User can open a local web page with text, title, voice/model, voice tuning, pause, and speed controls.
Submitting the form calls a server-side generation endpoint.
Generation endpoint loads ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, voice/model defaults, and ONEDRIVE_AUDIO_DIR from the environment.
Successful generation returns metadata with saved path, segment count, preview URL, and download URL.
Download and preview URLs serve only generated files from the configured output directory.
Errors are returned clearly without exposing the API key.
tasks.notes: Local stdlib web app, server-side ElevenLabs client, static UI, env template, and tests added.

completion_criteria: T001 is done, tests pass, changes are committed, and the branch is pushed.
